 Data structure to hold quadrature points and weights for the pair of triangles.
 

Detailed Description

Builds synchronized quadrature nodes/weights for a pair of touching triangles.

The class generates paired sampling points (barycentric) on the two triangles together with properly scaled weights so that singular and near-singular integrals (e.g., double-surface integrals with 1/R kernels) are integrable with high accuracy.

After construction, call:

  • getPointsMap(0) for triangle #1 nodes
  • getPointsMap(1) for triangle #2 nodes
  • getWeights() for the weights associated with each paired sample

Definition at line 40 of file SingCancellation.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ SingCancellation()

SingCancellation::SingCancellation ( int commonNodes,
Triangle * tPtr1,
Triangle * tPtr2,
int nQuadrature,
double(*) xQuadrature[2],
double * wQuadrature )

Construct and immediately generate the paired quadrature for a triangle pair.

Detects how the triangles touch by commonNodes and calls the appropriate builder:

  • 1 -> commonVertex()
  • 2 -> commonEdge()
  • 3 -> commonFacet()
Parameters
commonNodesNumber of common nodes (1, 2, or 3).
tPtr1Pointer to first triangle.
tPtr2Pointer to second triangle.
nQuadratureNumber of 1D GL points (typically 1, 4, or 8).
xQuadratureGL nodes array of shape [nQuadrature][2] with \( (\xi, 1-\xi) \).
wQuadratureGL weights array of length nQuadrature.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif commonNodes not in {1,2,3}.

Member Function Documentation

◆ commonEdge()

void SingCancellation::commonEdge ( )

Build paired quadrature for triangles sharing a common edge.

Produces \( 12 n^4 \) paired nodes per the six base mappings plus their symmetric counterparts ( \( \xi \leftrightarrow \eta \)). Includes the \( \omega \)-dependent Jacobian. Based on Taylor (2003), D'Elia et al. (2011), Sarraf et al. (2014).

Note
Called automatically by the constructor when commonNodes==2.

Build quadrature for triangles sharing a common edge. Mapping creates six base cases ( \(k=0...5\)), then symmetrizes to twelve, yielding \(12n^4\) paired nodes overall. The \(\omega\)-dependent Jacobian \( x_1 \omega^2 (1-\omega) \) is folded into the weights.

Warning
std::runtime_error if no common edge is found.

Definition at line 128 of file SingCancellation.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ commonFacet()

void SingCancellation::commonFacet ( )

Build paired quadrature for identical triangles (common facet).

Produces \( 6 n^4 \) paired nodes from three base mappings and symmetry, with per-case Jacobians folded into the weights. Based on Taylor (2003), D'Elia et al. (2011), Sarraf et al. (2014).

Note
Called automatically by the constructor when commonNodes==3.

Build quadrature for identical triangles (common facet). Uses three base mappings and symmetry ( \( \xi \leftrightarrow \eta \)) to produce \( 6 n^4 \) pairs. A per-case Jacobian (three values) is applied to the weights.

Definition at line 252 of file SingCancellation.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ commonVertex()

void SingCancellation::commonVertex ( )

Build paired quadrature for triangles sharing a single vertex.

Produces \( 2 n^4 \) paired nodes (symmetrization doubles the base \( n^4 \) set), with weights including the appropriate Jacobian factors arising from the four nested scalar transforms. Based on Taylor (2003), D'Elia et al. (2011), Sarraf et al. (2014).

Note
Called automatically by the constructor when commonNodes==1.

Build quadrature for triangles with one common vertex. Creates the raw 4D tensor-product rule ( \( n^4 \) points), then applies the two-way symmetrization ( \( \xi \leftrightarrow \eta \)) to obtain \( 2 n^4 \) paired samples. Each sample produces one barycentric triple on triangle #1 and the corresponding triple on triangle #2, plus the combined weight: \( w = 4 w_i w_j w_p w_q J(\xi, \eta, ...) \).

Internally uses Triangle::FindCommonVertexIndices() to determine how the barycentric coordinates must be permuted on each triangle so that the same physical vertex is aligned in both local parameterizations.

Warning
On failure to detect the shared vertex, a runtime_error is thrown.

Definition at line 38 of file SingCancellation.cpp.

◆ getPermutation()

std::vector< int > SingCancellation::getPermutation ( int shift)

Permutation of barycentric components induced by vertex/edge alignment.

The shift encodes how the \( (\lambda_1, \lambda_2, \lambda_3) \) triple must be permuted so that the first component corresponds to the vertex opposite the "free" entity.

Parameters
shiftEncoded shift (−3...−1, 1...3).
Returns
A permutation vector of indices in {0,1,2}.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumenton unsupported shift.

Map "shift code" -> permutation indices of a barycentric triple. Encodes the rotations/reflections needed to align local \( (\lambda_1, \lambda_2, \lambda_3) \) choices across the two triangles so the same physical vertex/edge is addressed.

Definition at line 357 of file SingCancellation.cpp.

triangleIndex0 for the first triangle, 1 for the second.

◆ writeMatlabMatrixToTxt()

void SingCancellation::writeMatlabMatrixToTxt ( const std::string & filename,
const std::string & varname,
const std::string & component,
const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & V,
int size,
int prec = 6 )

Write a single matrix column (chosen component) as a MATLAB style vector.

Writes: varname = [ v1; v2; ... ];

Parameters
filenameOutput text filename.
varnameMATLAB variable name to assign.
componentWhich barycentric component to export: "x","y","z" (0,1,2). If empty, writes the first component by default.
VMatrix to export (rows correspond to points).
sizeNumber of rows to write (typically V.size()).
precDecimal precision (default 6).
